title: Attention Is All You Need
why: Introduced the Transformer architecture — the foundation of every modern LLM, vision model, and multimodal system in use today.

title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
why: Established masked language modelling and bidirectional pretraining, shaping how we think about transfer learning in NLP.

title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners (GPT-3)
why: Demonstrated that scale alone unlocks emergent in-context learning — no fine-tuning required. Sparked the modern LLM era.

title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Vision Transformer (ViT)
why: Proved Transformers work as well on images as on text, unifying the architectures behind vision and language AI.

title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
why: The paper behind Stable Diffusion, DALL·E 2, and Midjourney. Essential reading for understanding modern image and video generation.

title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision (CLIP)
why: Showed that training on image-text pairs at scale yields powerful zero-shot vision models. The backbone of most multimodal systems.

title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback (InstructGPT)
why: Introduced RLHF as a practical alignment technique — the method that turned raw LLMs into useful assistants like ChatGPT.

title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition (ResNet)
why: Residual connections solved the vanishing gradient problem and enabled training of very deep networks — still fundamental today.

title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization
why: The default optimizer for training nearly every neural network. Understanding Adam is non-negotiable for practitioners.

title: Generative Adversarial Networks
why: Introduced GANs — one of the most influential ideas in generative AI, still widely used in image synthesis and data augmentation.

title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms (PPO)
why: The dominant RL algorithm used in RLHF pipelines and robotics. Simple, stable, and effective at scale.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks (RAG)
why: The foundational paper for RAG systems — now the standard approach for grounding LLMs with external knowledge.
